name: Springerle (Molded Christmas Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the eggs until very light and fluffy.
2. Gradually add sugar, beating for 15 minutes without underbeating.
3. Fold in anise extract and flour.
4. Roll dough to a thickness of 3/8 inch.
5. Thoroughly flour the springerle mold or rolling pin.
6. Press molds firmly into the dough.
7. Cut cookies apart and place on a greased and floured cookie sheet.
8. Let dry overnight at room temperature, covered with paper towels or uncovered.
9. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
10. Immediately reduce the temperature to 300°F (150°C) after placing the cookies in the oven.
11. Bake for 15 minutes. The cookies should not brown.
12. Store cookies for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the flavor to mellow.
13. These cookies can be used for dunking in coffee, tea, or cocoa.
14. For Christmas, paint designs with egg yolk colored with food coloring.
